name: Barbequed Beef Sandwiches
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons cider vinegar or white vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the meat, water, onion with cloves, and bay leaves in a pressure cooker.
2. Cook for 30 minutes, allowing the vent to cool down on its own.
3. Alternatively, double the amount of water and simmer in a heavy saucepot until tender (about 3 hours).
4. Remove the meat and reserve one cup of broth. Discard the onion and bay leaves.
5. Cut or shred the beef into bite-sized pieces.
6. In a small pan, melt butter and sauté chopped onion until tender and golden.
7. Add all remaining ingredients, along with the reserved broth, to the onions in a saucepan.
8. Simmer for 5-10 minutes.
9. Pour the sauce over the meat and simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
10. Serve on rolls or bread. (Keeps well in a crockpot; leftovers freeze well). Serves 10-12.
